questions.q: Rani Rudrama Devi ruled which dynasty's kingdom?
questions.explanation: Rudrama Devi governed the Kakatiya kingdom in her own right.

questions.q: From which capital city did Rudrama Devi rule?
questions.explanation: Rudrama Devi ruled the Kakatiya realm from its capital at Warangal.

questions.q: In which century CE did Rudrama Devi reign?
questions.explanation: Rudrama Devi governed the Kakatiya kingdom during the 13th century CE.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi was notable as one of the few women in Indian history to do what?
options: Rule a major kingdom in her own right
questions.explanation: She was among the rare women to rule a major Indian kingdom as sovereign, not merely as regent.

questions.q: Which famous Venetian traveller is said to have praised Rudrama Devi's rule?
questions.explanation: Marco Polo is traditionally credited with praising the queen's just governance.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi's kingdom was located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Kakatiya kingdom she ruled was centred on Warangal in present-day Telangana.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi is best remembered for primarily doing what during her reign?
questions.explanation: She is celebrated for defending the Kakatiya realm against internal and external rivals.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi was the daughter of which Kakatiya king?
questions.explanation: She was the daughter and chosen successor of the powerful Kakatiya king Ganapati Deva.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi is often depicted as which kind of ruler?
questions.explanation: She is remembered as a warrior queen who personally took part in defending her realm.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi belonged to which broad cultural-linguistic region of medieval India?
questions.explanation: As a Kakatiya ruler she belonged to the Telugu-speaking Deccan region.

questions.q: To succeed to the throne, Rudrama Devi was reportedly raised and presented in what manner?
options: As a son, under the name Rudradeva
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Ganapati Deva raised her as a son and she ruled under the masculine name Rudradeva.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi married into which family as part of a Kakatiya alliance?
questions.explanation: She was married to Virabhadra, of an Eastern Chalukya line, though she remained the ruling sovereign.

questions.q: Which neighbouring power was among the rivals Rudrama Devi had to repel?
questions.explanation: The Yadavas of Devagiri were among the Deccan rivals who challenged her kingdom.

questions.q: Who succeeded Rudrama Devi on the Kakatiya throne?
questions.explanation: Her grandson Prataparudra II succeeded her and became the last great Kakatiya ruler.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi is associated with strengthening which feature of Warangal?
questions.explanation: She is credited with continuing work on Warangal's fortifications to defend the capital.

questions.q: Marco Polo referred to the ruler of the kingdom he visited in glowing terms; he reached India during which broad period?
questions.explanation: Marco Polo travelled through southern India in the late 13th century, overlapping Rudrama Devi's era.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi is said to have died in connection with which activity?
options: A hunting accident in the Himalayas
questions.explanation: Accounts suggest she died while campaigning against a rebellious feudatory late in her reign.

questions.q: To manage her warrior chiefs, Rudrama Devi made use of which Kakatiya institution?
options: The iqta assignment of the Sultanate
questions.explanation: She relied on the Kakatiya nayaka system, binding warrior chiefs to the crown through land grants.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi is widely commemorated today; in 2015 she was the subject of which kind of tribute?
options: A space mission named after her
questions.explanation: A well-known Telugu historical film, 'Rudhramadevi', was released about her in 2015.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi's gender created opposition mainly from which quarter?
questions.explanation: Like several ruling queens, she faced resistance from nobles uneasy at a woman on the throne.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi is sometimes counted among India's notable ruling queens alongside which later figure?
questions.explanation: Razia Sultana of the Delhi Sultanate is another rare woman to rule in her own right in medieval India.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi's reign is dated approximately to which span?
questions.explanation: Her reign is conventionally dated to roughly the 1260s through to the late 1280s CE.

questions.q: Inscriptions show Rudrama Devi reigned using which regnal name to assert authority?
questions.explanation: She issued records under the masculine title Rudradeva Maharaja to reinforce her sovereignty.

questions.q: The Yadava ruler of Devagiri who clashed with Rudrama Devi's kingdom was from which line?
questions.explanation: The Seuna Yadavas of Devagiri were the Deccan rivals contending with the Kakatiyas in this period.

questions.q: The rebel against whom Rudrama Devi is traditionally said to have died was which chief?
questions.explanation: She is recorded as dying in conflict with the rebellious Kayastha chief Ambadeva around 1289 CE.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi formally took power around 1262 CE following the long reign of which predecessor?
questions.explanation: She succeeded after the lengthy reign of her father Ganapati Deva, who had nominated her.

questions.q: Marco Polo's account refers to the Kakatiya queen using which name for the kingdom or region?
questions.explanation: Marco Polo praised the queen of the realm linked to the port of Motupalli on the Kakatiya-controlled coast.

questions.q: The Motupalli inscription connected to Kakatiya rule is notable for being an early example of what?
options: A coronation oath of a queen
A grant of land to a poet
A charter protecting foreign maritime traders
questions.explanation: The Motupalli (Abhaya Sasana) charter offered protection and fair trade terms to seafaring merchants.

questions.q: The Motupalli merchant-protection charter that benefited Rudrama Devi's realm was originally issued by which Kakatiya ruler?
questions.explanation: Rudrama Devi's father Ganapati Deva issued the Motupalli Abhaya Sasana, guaranteeing fair trade terms to seafaring merchants.

questions.q: Rudrama Devi's example of female sovereignty in the Deccan is significant because such cases were?
questions.explanation: A woman ruling a major kingdom as full sovereign was exceptionally rare, making her reign historically notable.
